heaviest_rainfall.status: The heaviest total rainfall occurred on 09 November 2025, dropping 47.89mm of rain over the 144.8 hour storm duration with a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain rate registered stands at 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, highlighting an extreme precipitation event.  21.19mm fell during the storm over 15.0 hours.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 creating a new record that still lasts.  The storm produced a total of 47.89mm with a maximum intensity of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 23 on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: Stormy conditions are expected.  This is the first storm in the region since 02 January 2026.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is currently no cold snap.  This year there has been 1 cold snap, usually there are 3.6 cold snap per year.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C), set on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is currently no heatwaves.  This year there have been 0 heatwavess, usually there are 1.9 heatwavess per year.

tropical.status: The previous tropical night was on 18 July 2025, with only 62 in total since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (201°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The largest wind gust ever was on 13 October 2012 with a value of 93.7 mph.

run.status: The top recorded wind run is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: To date, the maximum wind speed recorded is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, a bit warmer for the feels like temperature (up 6.5°C), outside temperature (up 5.8°C), and inside temperature (up 2.5°C) and more moisture-laden outside humidity (up 7%) and rainfall (up 0.9 mm).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 415 m) and pressure (up 11 mbar).

The past week and month had observable variation in cloud levels (up 478 m), inside temperature (up 1.8°C), feels like temperature (down 4.8°C), and outside temperature (down 4.1°C) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 414.7m lower than yesterday, 477.6m lower than the past week, 418.1m lower than the past month.

The cloud levels for yesterday ranged from 708m to 1548m and averaged 710.0m, while the past week and month indicated warmer conditions.

summary.summary: Inside; cold temperatures are anticipated. Outside; cold spells are likely. Also, the atmosphere will feel parched and dry and calm and still conditions are expected. Showers, low clouds are expected, possibly some fog or persistent clouds, and it’s dark. Low pressure may bring unsettled weather.

washing.phrase: It is nighttime; if you have washing out then bring it in as it will probably get damper overnight.
